seo.title: CrewAI Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for CrewAI agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using CrewAI face heightened compliance risks due to multi-agent collaboration on customer-facing tasks like product recommendations, pricing, and personalized marketing. Agent delegation loops, hallucinated outputs, and task drift can lead to unsubstantiated claims, inaccurate pricing, or privacy violations, triggering FTC enforcement or CCPA fines. Vex provides essential guardrails by validating agent outputs against regulations at each task step.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims must be substantiated with evidence; misleading advertising can result in fines and lawsuits.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex checks agent-generated product descriptions and claims against a knowledge base of verified facts, flagging unsubstantiated statements before customer exposure.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data must be handled with explicit consent and minimal retention; breaches lead to massive fines.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits agent data access and PII usage in tasks, ensuring compliance with data minimization and consent requirements.

complianceRequirements.description: Payment card data must be securely processed without unnecessary exposure.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex monitors payment-related agent tasks to prevent hallucinated or exposed card details in outputs.

complianceRequirements.description: Website content and communications must be accessible to users with disabilities.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates generated marketing copy and UI suggestions for WCAG compliance, ensuring readable alt text and semantic structure.

industryRisks.name: Agent delegation loops
industryRisks.description: Agents endlessly pass hallucinated pricing data between roles, causing inconsistent checkout totals.
industryRisks.example: Marketing agent fabricates 50% discount claim, passed to checkout agent, resulting in refund disputes.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated tool outputs
industryRisks.description: Agents invent inventory or pricing data from faulty tool calls.
industryRisks.example: Product agent hallucinates 'out of stock' status, blocking legitimate sales.

industryRisks.name: Task drift
industryRisks.description: Agents reinterpret refund policy tasks, generating misleading customer assurances.
industryRisks.example: Support agent promises 'no-questions-asked lifetime returns' against actual 30-day policy.

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent output quality
industryRisks.description: Different crew roles produce varying compliance levels in product descriptions.
industryRisks.example: Copywriter agent includes ADA-inaccessible formatting while SEO agent does not.

implementation.title: Add Vex Callback to Product Recommendation Agent
implementation.description: Integrate Vex to validate product claims and pricing accuracy before recommendations reach customers.
implementation.code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

# Initialize Vex for e-commerce compliance
guardrail = VexGuardrail(api_key='your-vex-key')

product_agent = Agent(
    role='Product Recommender',
    goal='Recommend verified products with accurate pricing',
    backstory='Expert in FTC-compliant product recommendations',
    verbose=True
)

def vex_callback(task_output):
    return guardrail.validate({
        'type': 'product_claim',
        'content': task_output,
        'rules': ['ftc_substantiation', 'pricing_accuracy']
    })

recommend_task = Task(
    description='Recommend top 3 products for {customer_query}',
    agent=product_agent,
    callback=vex_callback
)

implementation.title: Guard Pricing Agent Against Hallucinations
implementation.description: Prevent pricing loops by validating real-time price data from tools against canonical sources.

implementation.title: Monitor Customer Data Tasks for CCPA Compliance
implementation.description: Audit personalization agents for proper PII handling and consent verification.

caseStudyScenario.heading: CrewAI in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
FashionCo deployed CrewAI to handle their Black Friday flash sale operations. A crew of five agents collaborated: Product Researcher gathered trending items, Pricing Specialist adjusted dynamic prices, Copywriter generated promotional content, Personalizer tailored recommendations, and Checkout Coordinator processed orders. Without guardrails, the Pricing Specialist began hallucinating inventory shortages due to a faulty API call, while the Copywriter fabricated 'limited time 70% off' claims unsubstantiated by actual discounts. The Personalizer accessed full customer profiles without consent verification, violating CCPA. Task drift occurred when Checkout Coordinator reinterpreted refund policies, promising 'no receipt needed' returns against store policy. This created a cascade of customer complaints, refund disputes, and FTC scrutiny.

The crew processed 10,000+ orders in 24 hours, but 15% resulted in pricing disputes and 8% triggered chargebacks from policy misrepresentations.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex callbacks on each agent task, FashionCo achieved 99.7% compliance across all Black Friday operations. Vex blocked 247 hallucinated pricing outputs, enforced FTC substantiation on 1,892 product claims, and redacted PII from 412 personalization tasks lacking consent. Agent delegation loops were terminated early when circular pricing references were detected. The crew maintained high throughput while eliminating compliance violations, resulting in zero FTC complaints and a 23% increase in customer trust scores. Revenue loss from disputes dropped from projected 7% to 0.2%.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK today and add compliance callbacks to your CrewAI agents in under 15 minutes.
